channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: One in ten unemployed who lost benefits in recent reforms has found work
item.description: Some 100,000 people have lost their unemployment benefits, with more waves due later this year and in 2027.

item.title: Ambassador welcomes "mutual affection" between Belgium and Japan
item.description: Japan’s emperor returns to Belgium for first state visit in 30 years, celebrating 160 years of diplomatic ties between two nations with shared values.

item.title: Japan imperial couple visit Belgium
item.description: Emperor Naruhito’s study of rivers shapes a unique visit to Belgium, linking diplomacy, history, and cutting-edge research.

item.title: Israel says it is striking Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes overnight from Thursday to Friday in southern Lebanon's Nabatieh region have killed at least 16 people.

item.title: After Iran, Trump wants to focus on North Korea, says South Korean president
item.description: Hours after announcing the Iran deal, Donald Trump posted an uncaptioned photo of his 2018 meeting with North Korean leader Kim Jong Un in Singapore.

item.title: FIFA removes nearly 400,000 hateful comments on social media
item.description: "We’re trying to educate young people so that they grow up to be empathetic individuals, not just people who love the sport. There is no place for discrimination in our society."

item.title: US and Iran sign provisional agreement to end war
item.description: The 'memorandum of understanding' stipulates that the Strait of Hormuz will be reopened and that all conflicts in the region will be brought to an end.

item.title: Decathlon offers €2,000 worth of free shares to employees worldwide
item.description: Decathlon employs around 103,000 people globally and operates 1,902 stores worldwide.

item.title: Swedish church launches investigation into Prime Minister's wife
item.description: The Church of Sweden is investigating the Swedish Prime Minister's wife, who is a priest, following allegations about her foundation.

item.title: G7 'agrees to ramp up pressure' on Russia through energy 'sanctions'
item.description: The leaders of the G7, meeting in France, have agreed to step up pressure on Russia through sanctions on hydrocarbons.

item.title: North Macedonia: Two Bulgarian Embassy cars set on fire
item.description: Two cars belonging to the Embassy of Bulgaria were set on fire on Monday outside the embassy in Skopje, the Interior Ministry of North Macedonia reported.

item.title: 'I believe France owes me a debt': French man, 104, demands payment for forced labour during WWII
item.description: The European Court of Human Rights will now decide whether the case is admissible.

item.title: Norwegian crown princess's son jailed for committing two rapes
item.description: The scandal first erupted in August 2024 when Høiby was arrested on suspicion of assaulting a partner at a residence in an affluent district of Oslo.

item.title: Iranian Supreme Leader's funeral scheduled for 4 July
item.description: Iran schedules its Supreme Leader’s funeral for July, months after war postponed proceedings. The burial’s timing aligns with a notable US milestone: 250 years of independence.

item.title: Iran rules out resolution of peace talks
item.description: “Closer than ever” or not quite there? As mediator signals hope, Iran clarifies there’s no peace accord expected by tomorrow.

item.title: Drone-disrupting jammers to be used at nuclear plants
item.description: Belgium widens use of jammers for nuclear sites, prisons, and hazardous facilities — but strict conditions aim to limit risks to third parties.

item.title: Trump's name removed from Kennedy Center
item.description: The Kennedy Center’s link to Trump has officially been severed. A judge ruled renaming of the historic venue invalid, reinstating its original dedication to John F. Kennedy.

item.title: Peace agreement between Iran and United States can be finalised in 24 hours
item.description: Pakistan prepares for potential peace pact between Iran and US within 24 hours, but new clashes underscore lingering uncertainties.

item.title: Belgian company entrusted with visuals for NBA finals
item.description: Madison Square Garden becomes a canvas for Belgian creators as the Knicks pursue their first NBA finals victory in over two decades.

item.title: Sweden intercepts Russian fighter jets over Baltic Sea
item.description: Russian aircraft tested Sweden’s borders on Friday, prompting swift NATO-aligned responses. The Baltic region remains a flashpoint as tensions surge post-Ukraine invasion.
